{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 95,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Pipe shoe is completely off its intended structural support.",
    "Load path is interrupted, leaving pipe segment unsupported or eccentrically supported.",
    "Visible gap/offset between bottom of shoe and top of steel, risk of pipe sag, overstress, and flange leakage."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately barricade area and restrict access below unsupported pipe.",
    "Lift or jack pipe to relieve unintended stress, then reposition and secure shoe onto support.",
    "Verify support steel alignment, bolt torque, and shim packs per support standard.",
    "Re-inspect adjacent hangers for cascading overload."
  ],
  "rationale": "A pipe shoe completely off its support constitutes an installed-defective and non-compliant condition. Under MSS SP-58 and project support standards, support gaps or missing bearing break the intended load path. In a heavy-industrial setting with rough-complete piping, this is a high-risk structural/safety defect that requires immediate correction before hydrotest or service.",
  "references": ["MSS SP-58", "project support standard"]
}
